sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , Κάδμου τοῦ πάλαι νέα τροφή ,
sections.translation: My children , latest-born wards of old Cadmus , why do you sit before me like this with wreathed branches of suppliants , while the city reeks with incense ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: ὁμοῦ δὲ παιάνων τε καὶ στεναγμάτων ·
sections.translation: rings with prayers for health and cries of woe ? I thought it unbefitting , my children , to hear these things from the mouths of others , and have come here myself , I , Oedipus renowned by all . Tell me , then , venerable old man — since it is proper that you

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: πρὸ τῶνδε φωνεῖν , τίνι τρόπῳ καθέστατε ,
sections.translation: speak for these — in what mood you sit here , one of fear or of desire ? Be sure that I will gladly give you all my help . I would be hard-hearted indeed if I did not pity such suppliants as these .

sections.label: Section 14
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὦ κρατύνων Οἰδίπους χώρας ἐμῆς ,
sections.translation: Oedipus , ruler of my land , you see the age of those who sit

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ὁρᾷς μὲν ἡμᾶς ἡλίκοι προσήμεθα
sections.translation: on your altars — some , nestlings still too tender for flight , others , bowed with age , priests , like me of Zeus , and some , these here , the chosen youth . The rest of the folk sit

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ἀγοραῖσι θακεῖ πρός τε Παλλάδος διπλοῖς
sections.translation: with wreathed branches in the market-place , and before the shrines of Pallas , and where Ismenus gives answer by fire . For the city , as you yourself see , is now sorely vexed , and can no longer lift her head from beneath the angry waves of death .

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: φθίνουσα μὲν κάλυξιν ἐγκάρποις χθονός ,
sections.translation: A blight has fallen on the fruitful blossoms of the land , the herds among the pastures , the barren pangs of women . And the flaming god , the malign plague , has swooped upon us , and ravages the town : he lays waste to the house of Cadmus , but enriches Hades with

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: Ἅιδης στεναγμοῖς καὶ γόοις πλουτίζεται .
sections.translation: groans and tears . It is not because we rank you with the gods that I and these children are suppliants at your hearth , but because we deem you the first among men in life ’ s common fortunes and in dealings with the divinities :

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: ὅς γʼ ἐξέλυσας ἄστυ Καδμεῖον μολὼν
sections.translation: when you came to the city of the Cadmeans , you freed us from the tax that we rendered to the hard songstress , and that when you knew no more than anyone else , nor had you been taught , but rather by the assistance of a god , as the story goes , you uplifted our life .

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: νῦν τʼ , ὦ κράτιστον πᾶσιν Οἰδίπου κάρα ,
sections.translation: Now , Oedipus , king glorious in our eyes , we , your suppliants , beseech you to find some defence for us , whether you hear it from some divine omen , or learn of it from some mortal . For I see that the outcome of the councils of experienced men

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ζώσας ὁρῶ μάλιστα τῶν βουλευμάτων .
sections.translation: most often have effect . On , best of mortals , uplift our state ! On , guard your fame , since now this land calls you savior on account of your former zeal . Let us not remember of your reign that

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: στάντες τʼ ἐς ὀρθὸν καὶ πεσόντες ὕστερον .
sections.translation: we were first restored and then cast down : lift this state so that it falls no more ! With good omen you provided us that past happiness : show yourself the same now too , since if you are to rule this land just as you do now , it is better to be lord of men than of a wasteland .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ξὺν ἀνδράσιν κάλλιον ἢ κενῆς κρατεῖν ·
sections.translation: Neither walled town nor ship is anything , if it is empty and no men dwell within .

sections.label: Section 58
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες οἰκτροί , γνωτὰ κοὐκ ἄγνωτά μοι
sections.translation: My piteous children , I know quite well the desires with which you have come : I know well that

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: νοσεῖτε πάντες , καὶ νοσοῦντες , ὡς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: you all are sick , and though you are sick I know well that there is not one of you who is as sick as I . Your pain comes on each of you for himself alone , and for no other , but my soul is in pain at once for the city , for myself , and for you .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: ὥστʼ οὐχ ὕπνῳ γʼ εὕδοντά μʼ ἐξεγείρετε ,
sections.translation: Thus you are not awakening me from sleep : no , be sure that I have wept many tears , wandered far and wide in my thought . I have made use of the only remedy which I could find after close consideration : I sent my relative

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: Κρέοντʼ , ἐμαυτοῦ γαμβρόν , ἐς τὰ Πυθικὰ
sections.translation: Creon , Menoeceus ’ son , to Apollo ’ s Pythian residence to learn what we might do or say to protect this city . And now , when the lapse of days is reckoned , I am troubled about what he is doing , for he has been away an unreasonably long time

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἄπεστι πλείω τοῦ καθήκοντος χρόνου .
sections.translation: beyond what is fitting . But when he arrives , I would be no true man if did not perform all that the god reveals .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰς καλὸν σύ τʼ εἶπας οἵδε τʼ ἀρτίως
sections.translation: You have spoken opportunely , since at this time these people here indicate that Creon is drawing near .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ὦναξ Ἄπολλον , εἰ γὰρ ἐν τύχῃ γέ τῳ
sections.translation: Lord Apollo , may he come to us in the brightness of saving fortune , even as his face is bright !

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰκάσαι μέν , ἡδύς · οὐ γὰρ ἂν κάρα
sections.translation: He seems to bring comfort , since otherwise he would not be coming crowned so thickly with berry-laden bay .

sections.label: Section 84
sections.original: τάχʼ εἰσόμεσθα · ξύμμετρος γὰρ ὡς κλύειν .
sections.translation: We will soon know : he is within hearing-range .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ἄναξ , ἐμὸν κήδευμα , παῖ Μενοικέως ,
sections.translation: Prince , my kinsman , child of Menoeceus , what news have you brought us from the god ?

sections.label: Section 87
sections.original: ἐσθλήν · λέγω γὰρ καὶ τὰ δύσφορʼ , εἰ τύχοι
sections.translation: Good news . I tell you that even troubles hard to bear will end in perfect peace if they find the right issue .

sections.label: Section 89
sections.original: ἔστιν δὲ ποῖον τοὔπος ; οὔτε γὰρ θρασὺς
sections.translation: But what is the oracle ? So far , your words

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: οὔτʼ οὖν προδείσας εἰμὶ τῷ γε νῦν λόγῳ .
sections.translation: neither encourage nor frighten me .

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: εἰ τῶνδε χρῄζεις πλησιαζόντων κλύειν ,
sections.translation: If you want to hear in the presence of these people , I am ready to speak : otherwise we can go inside .

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: ἐς πάντας αὔδα · τῶνδε γὰρ πλέον φέρω
sections.translation: Speak to all . The sorrow that I bear for these is more than for my own life .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: λέγοιμʼ ἂν οἷʼ ἤκουσα τοῦ θεοῦ πάρα .
sections.translation: I will tell you what I heard from the god . Phoebus our lord clearly commands us to drive out the defilement which he said was harbored in this land , and not to nourish it so that it cannot be healed .

sections.label: Section 99
sections.original: ποίῳ καθαρμῷ ; τίς ὁ τρόπος τῆς ξυμφορᾶς ;
sections.translation: With what sort of purification ? What is the manner of the misfortune ?

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: ἀνδρηλατοῦντας ἢ φόνῳ φόνον πάλιν
sections.translation: By banishing the man , or by paying back bloodshed with bloodshed , since it is this blood which brings the tempest on our city .

sections.label: Section 102
sections.original: ποίου γὰρ ἀνδρὸς τήνδε μηνύει τύχην ;
sections.translation: And who is the man whose fate he thus reveals ?

sections.label: Section 103
sections.original: ἦν ἡμίν , ὦναξ , Λάϊός ποθʼ ἡγεμὼν
sections.translation: Laius , my lord , was the leader of our land before you assumed control of this state .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: ἔξοιδʼ ἀκούων · οὐ γὰρ εἰσεῖδόν γέ πω .
sections.translation: I know it well — by hearsay , for I never saw him .

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: τούτου θανόντος νῦν ἐπιστέλλει σαφῶς
sections.translation: He was slain , and the god now bids us to take vengeance on his murderers , whoever they are .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: οἳ δʼ εἰσὶ ποῦ γῆς ; ποῦ τόδʼ εὑρεθήσεται
sections.translation: Where on earth are they ? Where shall the dim track of this old crime be found ?

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: ἐν τῇδʼ ἔφασκε γῇ · τὸ δὲ ζητούμενον
sections.translation: In this land , the god said . What is sought for can be caught ; only that which is not watched escapes .

sections.label: Section 112
sections.original: πότερα δʼ ἐν οἴκοις ἢ ʼν ἀγροῖς ὁ Λάϊος
sections.translation: Was it in the house , or in the field , or on foreign soil that Laius met his bloody end ?

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: θεωρός , ὡς ἔφασκεν , ἐκδημῶν , πάλιν
sections.translation: He left our land , as he said , on a mission to Delphi .

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: πρὸς οἶκον οὐκέθʼ ἵκεθʼ , ὡς ἀπεστάλη .
sections.translation: And once he had set forth , he never again returned .

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἄγγελός τις οὐδὲ συμπράκτωρ ὁδοῦ
sections.translation: And was there none to tell ? Was there no travelling companion who saw the deed , from whom tidings might have been gained , and used ?

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: θνῄσκουσι γάρ , πλὴν εἷς τις , ὃς φόβῳ , φυγὼν
sections.translation: All perished , save one who fled in fear , and he could tell with assurance only one thing of all that he saw .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: τὸ ποῖον ; ἓν γὰρ πόλλʼ ἂν ἐξεύροι μαθεῖν ,
sections.translation: And what was that ? One thing might hold the clue to many , if we could only get a small beginning for hope .

sections.label: Section 122
sections.original: λῃστὰς ἔφασκε συντυχόντας οὐ μιᾷ
sections.translation: He said that robbers fell upon them , not one man alone , but with a great force .

sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ὁ λῃστής , εἴ τι μὴ ξὺν ἀργύρῳ
sections.translation: How then , unless some intrigue had been worked with bribes

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: ἐπράσσετʼ ἐνθένδʼ , ἐς τόδʼ ἂν τόλμης ἔβη ;
sections.translation: from here in Thebes , would the robbers have been so bold ?

sections.label: Section 126
sections.original: δοκοῦντα ταῦτʼ ἦν · Λαΐου δʼ ὀλωλότος
sections.translation: Such things were surmised . But once Laius was slain no avenger arose in the midst of our troubles .

sections.label: Section 128
sections.original: κακὸν δὲ ποῖον ἐμποδών , τυραννίδος
sections.translation: But when royalty had fallen in this way , what trouble prevented a full search ?

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: ἡ ποικιλῳδὸς Σφὶγξ τὸ πρὸς ποσὶν σκοπεῖν
sections.translation: The riddling Sphinx had forced us to let things that were obscure go , and to investigate the pressing trouble .

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξ ὑπαρχῆς αὖθις αὔτʼ ἐγὼ φανῶ ·
sections.translation: I will start afresh , and once more make dark things plain . Worthily has Phoebus Apollo — and worthily have you — bestowed this care on behalf of the dead . And so , as is fitting , you will find me allied with you

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐνδίκως ὄψεσθε κἀμὲ σύμμαχον
sections.translation: in seeking vengeance for this land , and for the god as well . I will dispel this taint not on behalf of far-off friends , but for my own benefit . For whoever killed Laius

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: κἄμʼ ἂν τοιαύτῃ χειρὶ τιμωροῦνθʼ ἕλοι .
sections.translation: might wish to take vengeance on me also with a hand as fierce . Avenging Laius , therefore , I serve myself . Come , my children , as quickly as possible rise from the altar-steps , and lift these suppliant boughs . Let someone summon here Cadmus ’ people , warning them that I will leave nothing untried .

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: ὡς πᾶν ἐμοῦ δράσοντος · ἢ γὰρ εὐτυχεῖς
sections.translation: For with the god ’ s help our good fortune — or our ruin — will be made certain .

sections.label: Section 147
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ἱστώμεσθα · τῶνδε γὰρ χάριν
sections.translation: My children , let us rise . What we came to seek , this man promises of his own accord . And may Phoebus , who sent these oracles ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: σωτήρ δʼ ἵκοιτο καὶ νόσου παυστήριος .
sections.translation: come to us as savior and deliverer from the pestilence .

sections.label: Section 1.151
sections.original: ὦ Διὸς ἁδυεπὲς φάτι , τίς ποτε τᾶς πολυχρύσου
sections.translation: O sweetly-speaking message of Zeus , in what spirit have you come to glorious Thebes from golden Pytho ? I am on the rack , terror shakes my soul , O Delian healer to whom wild cries rise ,

sections.label: Section 1.155
sections.original: ἀμφὶ σοὶ ἁζόμενος τί μοι ἢ νέον
sections.translation: in holy fear of you , wondering what debt you will extract from me , perhaps unknown before , perhaps renewed with the revolving years . Tell me , immortal Voice , child of golden Hope .

sections.label: Section 1.160
sections.original: πρῶτα σὲ κεκλόμενος , θύγατερ Διός , ἄμβροτʼ Ἀθάνα
sections.translation: and on your sister , Artemis , guardian of our earth , who sits on her glorious throne above the circle of our market-place , and on far-shooting Apollo : oh shine forth for me , my three-fold help against death !

sections.label: Section 1.165
sections.original: εἴ ποτε καὶ προτέρας ἄτας ὕπερ ὀρνυμένας πόλει
sections.translation: If ever before you drove a fiery pest from our borders to stop ruin rushing upon our city , come now also !

sections.label: Section 2.169
sections.original: ὦ πόποι , ἀνάριθμα γὰρ φέρω
sections.translation: Alas , countless are the sorrows I bear .
